creator: rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru
text:
Wed, Jan 09, 2008 at 11:12:21AM -0800, Jo Rhett wrote:
> On Jan 9, 2008, at 10:53 AM, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
>> May be.  But why are you telling that BDB >= 4.4 is needed?
>> Do you have some negative experience?
> 
> Well the configure script says explicitly
>   AC_MSG_ERROR(This release of cfengine requires a version of BerkeleyDB 
> 4.4 or later)

;)) But even the configure from SVN trunk looks for 'db-4 db4
db-4.6 db-4.5 db-4.4 db-4.3 db-4.2 db-4.1 db-4.0'.  So, the configure
contradicts with itself.

>>>> Ah, that's the crucial part.  If true, then it's patched at the source.
>> 
>> Yes, it was patched.  And this was mentioned in the follow-up to the PR.=
> 
> I have the lastest trunk, and I don't see this.

If 'this' means 'the patches that fix the issue with older BDB', then
  http://svn.iu.hio.no/viewvc/trunk/src/instrument.c?root=Cfengine-2&r1=401&r2=402
  http://svn.iu.hio.no/viewvc/trunk/src/cfshow.c?root=Cfengine-2&r1=418&r2=433

>> I am submitting patches both to the current port and to the maintainer
>> of the mainline code.  And I am trying to push both of them, because
>> the port for the next version can be delayed (or the next version
>> can be delayed by itself) and I prefer the ports to make their job.
> 
> I disagree.  And this has been debated extensively on the ports list, and 
> consensus was that freebsd should not try to interfere with the maintainer. 

Didn't know about this.  A link to the discuission, please?

>    Also note that both 2.2.2 and 2.2.3 have come out since you reported 
> this, so frequency of updates to the mainline code base is not a problem.

Hmm, I hope that the situation with this PR was an exception: it
lied unanswered for 5 months and the port was not updated for the
same time interval..

>> it.  I had sent another patch to Mark some months ago and he promised
>> to analyze it once he will have time.  I will check for the state of
>> that patch and if it still not brought to the mainline code, I will
>> sent it to you, if you will be so kind to review it and push to the
>> sources.
> 
> You need to submit to bug-cfengine@cfengine.org, which is where patches are 
> dealt with.

Will do, thanks!
-- 
Eygene
